title: Generalize Work Cell execution without enlarging the Cell

mainline.contradiction: The generic Cell executes well in isolation, but execution modes beyond the fixed Swarm would duplicate scheduling, settlement, and evidence logic or leak orchestration vocabulary into CellInput.
acceptance: CellInput, CellDriver, and runCell remain free of queue, graph, priority, retry, and domain-planning vocabulary.
A common orchestration protocol can express current Swarm behavior and has explicit extension seams for bounded queues and dependency graphs.
Raw CellRunRecord and orchestration events remain sources while indexes, summaries, and queue views are rebuildable projections.
The current Swarm public behavior remains verified after the smallest kernel extraction.

branches.purpose: Design the orchestration boundary and migrate current Swarm onto the smallest reusable execution kernel.
branches.returnCondition: An adopted design candidate, preserved Swarm behavior, and action/boundary/context evidence are ready for independent review.
branches.mainlineDelta: Decision 031, the WorkSource kernel, open in-memory queue, Swarm projection, fatal-protocol evidence retention, and deterministic probes passed independent correction review.

branches.purpose: Probe AI SDK 7 migration and identify scoped context, observation, timeout, artifact projection, and terminal-semantics deltas without changing the core Cell contract.
branches.returnCondition: A bounded migration result records API changes, deterministic compatibility, one low-cost live observation, and a separate disposition for terminal semantics.
branches.mainlineDelta: Decision 032 migrated the replaceable adapter to AI SDK 7 and DeepSeek provider 3, retained performance evidence, and passed deterministic plus low-cost live provider probes.

branches.purpose: Resolve whether a caller-defined terminal tool ends the Cell immediately when terminalTools, outputSchema, and artifacts remain independent completion contracts.
branches.returnCondition: A contract decision and real adapter probe define simultaneous terminal/output behavior without a hardcoded final tool or an unnecessary recovery loop.
branches.mainlineDelta: Decision 033 defines orthogonal terminal, structured-output, and artifact semantics; live reviewer runs exposed and then verified primary-loop terminal submission, context-bearing recovery, and exactly-one output-step behavior.
